9 MONITORING AND REVIEW

This Policy is developed and owned by the  Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.

IS Team shall initiate a review of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment.

Feedback, suggestions, or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the CISO at CISO@.net.

2 INTRODUCTION

Security operations  to the combination of information security (IS) and IT operations to improve collaboration and reduce risks.

This Security Operations Policy (“Policy”) defines how Security Operations will be planned, established, implemented, operated, monitored, reviewed, maintained, and improved within . This Policy and its supporting policies set the premise for the organization's objective of safeguarding its information and underlying assets against internal and extern

6.4 Purchasing, Installation, Allocation and Management of New IT Systems

shall ensure that:

1. All purchases of software, IT equipment and peripherals shall follow a standard evaluation process, documented and done using approved vendors only.
2. All software and hardware installations shall be tested prior to being moved to live production environment by both IT and business users. Any changes to software or hardware configuration shall be appropriately authorized.
3. Capacity requirements s

5 POLICY STATEMENT

shall develop, implement, and maintain a Security Operations Policy ("Policy")  protection of assets through secure operational processes thereby contributing to an overall safe and secure organization.

5 POLICY STATEMENT

shall develop, implement, and maintain a Security Operations Policy ("Policy")  protection of assets through secure operational processes thereby contributing to an overall safe and secure organization.

8 POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS

All employees, contractors and third parties are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. In addition, violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the 's Human Capital Department, considering the recommendation of IS Team and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws, and regulations.

Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to disc

6.6 Segregation of Duties

shall:

1. Adopt the principle of segregation of duties to the maximum extent possible.
2. Include compensating controls such as monitoring of activities, maintenance and review of audit trails and management supervision where segregation of duties is not possible or practical.

6.1 Documenting the Operating Procedures

shall ensure that the:

1. IT Team ensures that Operating procedures and work instructions for all information systems and standards are:
2. Reviewed and updated periodically when there are:
3. Changes to Equipment/Systems Technology and Configurations.
4. Changes to business services.
4. Release Management.
5. Software Development.
6. Problem Management.
7. Incident Management.
4. IT Team ensures that procedures are kept up-to-date are available to the

6.5 System Acceptance

shall ensure that:

1. System acceptance tests are done in an appropriate test environment.
2. Before moving any new system into production, it shall be tested as per the System Acceptance Plan.
2. ’s system performance requirements.
3. ’s Security requirements.
4. ’s business continuity requirements.
5. Error recovery and restart procedures.
6. Effects of the new system on other existing systems.
7. User training requirements and ease of use of new systems, so as to ensur

shall develop, implement, and maintain a Security Operations Policy ("Policy")  protection of assets through secure operational processes thereby contributing to an overall safe and secure organization.

-IS-POL-0

Abstract

This Security Operations Policy sets the premise to ensure secure and effective operations in order to protect and safeguard  information and information systems.

**STATEMENT OF CONFIDENTIALITY**

All information in this document is  PRIVILEGED and for INTERNAL USE ONLY.

The information in this document may contain  proprietary trade secrets and confidential information, the disclosure of which could substantially benefit competitors. As a result, this docu

6.2 Controls ver System Documentation

shall ensure that:

1. System documentation is kept under safe custody.
2. Access is restricted to only those who have a business need and are authorized.
3. Version control is implemented to ensure that the current version is consistently available.
4. Copying of any system documentation shall be controlled and shall be allowed only after authorization.
5. The respective custodians of documents shall hold the original set of the system documentation.

5 POLICY STATEMENT

shall develop, implement, and maintain a Security Operations Policy ("Policy")  protection of assets through secure operational processes thereby contributing to an overall safe and secure organization.

6.4 Purchasing, Installation, Allocation and Management of New IT Systems

shall ensure that:

1. All purchases of software, IT equipment and peripherals shall follow a standard evaluation process, documented and done using approved vendors only.
2. All software and hardware installations shall be tested prior to being moved to live production environment by both IT and business users. Any changes to software or hardware configuration shall be appropriately authorized.

6.3 Separation of Development, Test and Operational Environments

shall ensure that:

1. Production environment shall be logically separated from test and development environments.
2. Access to each environment shall be controlled using firewalls.
3. Transfer of software between the development, test and production environments shall be subject to specific procedures such as the  Information Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance Procedure and will be documented.
4. Development staff s
